Layer 1 run, with real inputs

One scout run, decoded.

Follow the actual data packet from market slice to tool receipts, model output, persistent map memory, geometry, and the next research instruction.

What this isA first-layer scout: one narrow research cell, not a finished trade call.
What it sawA timestamped cell, selected atlas tools, four receipts, and prior map context.
What happened10/10 layers passed. 5/18 data surfaces touched. Geometry said: widen scouts.
Swipe walkthrough

Read this like a deck.

Swipe card by card. Each card says what went in, what came out, and which real receipt proves it happened.

Swipe sideways
Start1

A market cell is born.

Input: market universe, horizon grid, lens rules, themes, and coverage state. Output: one typed research cell that says exactly what this scout is allowed to care about.

cellHYPE / intraday / on_chain / frontier
themevalidator_unstake
clock2026-05-22T00:44:04.782678+00:00
Policy2

The research genome shapes the cell.

Input: the active MarketEvolve program. Output: prompt variant, tool menu width, evidence budget, source targets, and experiment lineage stamped onto the cell.

prompt varianttemporal_pyramid_v1
tool menu4 exposed URIs
policy score59%
Tools3

Receipts arrive before opinion.

Input: the cell plus approved atlas candidates. Output: compact tool receipts with tool_call_log IDs that the model can cite and the monitor can audit.

evidence calls4
receipt idstc_live_events, tc_live_hydro, tc_live_node
source mixEvent feed, Hydromancer actor quality, Our HL node rejects, +1 more
Prompt4

The model is boxed into a contract.

Input: system contract, user packet, receipts, prior strings, and allowed tools. Output: strict JSON only, with hypothesis, strings, scores, refs, and tool requests.

modeldeepseek:v4-flash
confidence64%
qualityprompt_variant:temporal_pyramid_v1, prompt_quality:0.74
Memory5

The output becomes map memory.

Input: parsed model JSON. Output: durable rows for the hypothesis, information strings, event intelligence, node intelligence, evidence refs, and tool proposals.

scout idscout_b3fcf55687
hypothesis idhyp_570afd6b62
string idsistr_f0e312583eb1d483, istr_b3cea3c709ae1cbc, istr_fb224cd52c6d3154
Attention6

Many strings compete for budget.

Input: recent information strings for the cycle. Output: confluences, tensions, promoted string IDs, and a smaller verifier-ready candidate set.

synthesisisyn_b93cdaf363a4
strings read2
promoted1
Shape7

The map forms a visible geometry.

Input: stored strings and their source families. Output: market cells with coordinates, scores, and a route directive that tells the next layer what to do.

top routewiden scouts
trade scream62%
readiness90%
Learn8

The system edits its future behavior.

Input: cycle metrics, geometry, tool usage, and learned-tool outcomes. Output: an evaluator score, a candidate mutation, and a hard experiment plan.

mutationexploit_learned_tool_surface
whyLearned tools are producing successful evidence calls; bias future scout tool surfaces toward them.
data coverage18 surfaces tracked
01 / why this layer exists

The market is too wide for one prompt.

Layer 1 solves that by running many narrow scouts. Each scout gets one receptive field, a small tool budget, and a strict output contract. The goal is not instant genius; the goal is high-quality coverage that compounds into a shared map.

1

Cut the market into cells

Each scout receives one entity, horizon, lens, bias, theme, and clock.

2

Fetch evidence before reasoning

The desk scores the full approved atlas, selects a tiny call budget, and attaches compact receipts.

3

Prompt against a contract

The model must return JSON with thesis, mechanism, kill signal, scores, and refs.

4

Validate the response

Weak strings, invented tools, missing refs, and uncalibrated scores are waste signals.

5

Route to memory

Every useful finding enters the information map as evidence, strings, nodes, edges, and missing-edge prompts.

The reusable field of view

Every scout is framed by the same six fields. That makes thousands of cheap calls comparable, replayable, and measurable.

Entitythe object being sensed
Horizonthe expiry window
Lensthe allowed data universe
Biasthe scout posture
Themethe causal pattern
Clockthe freshness boundary
Layer 1 is allowed to say: "inside this cell, these receipts imply this conditional string."
Layer 1 is not allowed to say: "I generally feel bullish or bearish."
02 / the real input packet

Here is exactly what this scout was handed.

The scout did not receive a vague request to be smart. It received a precise market cell, a clock, a scored subset of approved tools, and evidence receipts that can be audited later.

Assigned cell

This is the scout's world for the call.

EntityHYPE
Horizonintraday
Lenson_chain
Biasfrontier
Themevalidator_unstake
Clock2026-05-22T00:44:04.782678+00:00

Selected atlas slice

The scout can draw from the approved read-only atlas. This call exposes the relevant subset and keeps spend under a hard cap.

tic://tool/builtin/query_events_recent@v1tic://tool/hydromancer/get_hl_pnl_leaderboard@v1tic://tool/builtin/hl_reject_corpus@v1tic://tool/builtin/query_timeseries@v1
03 / the bigger data substrate

The scout sees a selected slice of a much larger estate.

This proof run touched only part of the data layer. That is intentional: the harness exposes the most relevant sources first, records what was touched, and names the important surfaces still missing. More data only helps when it closes a map edge.

5/18data surfaces touched by this run
7active receipt links
6typed edges formed
53%substrate coverage score
Touched

Event feed

What happened. Did a fresh event enter the market clock?

Receipts: tc_live_events

Touched

Market state

Can it matter. What do depth, open interest, funding, and tape context say?

Receipts: tc_live_events, tc_live_market

Touched

Hydromancer actor graph

Who is involved. Are the actors skilled, noisy, crowded, or historically predictive?

Receipts: tc_live_hydro

Touched

Our HL node

What our node knows. Do our own rejects, fills, and state deltas show healthy or stressed flow?

Receipts: tc_live_node

Touched

Options, vol, and derivatives

How risk is priced. Do IV, skew, funding, OI, liquidations, or basis confirm/crowd the claim?

Receipts: tc_live_events, tc_live_hydro

available

Builder flow

Where orders route. Which builders, users, and coins are carrying the flow?

available

Wallet route state

Where supply goes. Does the actor restake, bridge, idle, route to CEX, or hit sellable liquidity?

tool gap

Mempool and pending intent

What may happen next. Are pending transactions or router interactions visible before settlement?

available

Source health and citations

Can we trust it. Are sources fresh, resolved, and citation-addressable?

04 / connection expansion

The next call is chosen by the missing connection.

The system should not spend calls just to feel busy. It should ask: which missing edge would change the map? Actor route, builder flow, source health, pending intent, order-flow expression. If the edge needs a tool we do not have, the gap becomes a tool proposal.

event -> HYPE market_stateformed by this run
event -> actor_qualityformed by this run
wallet -> node_order_qualityformed by this run
event -> node_receiptsformed by this run
order_state -> absorption_contextformed by this run
event -> vol_positioningformed by this run
high priority

Resolve actor route state

The scout knows a HYPE event exists, but not where the actor routes after the event.

Edge: event -> wallet_route -> sellable_liquidity

tic://tool/learned/wallet_route_state_monitor@v1 (needs atlas/proposal)
high priority

Join builder flow

Builder fills can reveal whether the flow is concentrated, informed, or mechanically routed.

Edge: wallet -> builder -> fills

tic://tool/hydromancer/get_builder_fills@v1 (needs atlas/proposal)
high priority

Resolve source health and citations

A high-quality string should know whether every receipt is fresh and addressable.

Edge: claim -> citation -> source_health

tic://tool/builtin/query_source_health@v1 (needs atlas/proposal)tic://tool/learned/evidence_ref_resolver@v1 (needs atlas/proposal)
medium priority

Watch pending intent

Pending router or contract interactions can turn node intelligence from reactive to early.

Edge: pending_tx -> actor_intent -> event

tic://tool/learned/hyperevm_mempool_actor_watch@v1 (needs atlas/proposal)
05 / what the scout sees

The prompt is the model's cockpit.

The user prompt carries the cell, clock, evidence, prior strings, and allowed tools. The system prompt carries the contract. Together they make the model operate like an instrument instead of an open-ended chat.

Real prompt excerpt

This is the scout's actual input surface: timestamp, cell, evidence, and permitted tools.

as_of_utc=2026-05-22T00:44:04.782678+00:00
Freshness rule: for intraday/1d/1w horizons, do not use old historical dates as live catalyst evidence unless explicitly labeled as regime context.
Cell:
  entity=HYPE
  horizon=intraday
  lens=on_chain
  bias_mode=frontier
  theme=validator_unstake

tool_evidence:
  - tic://tool/builtin/query_events_recent@v1 ok=True tool_call_log_id=tc_live_events summary={"events": [{"entity": "HYPE", "event_time": "2026-05-21T11:30:00Z", "event_type": "unstake", "headline": "Upcoming unstaking aHYPE 558.1K HYPE in 3h.", "metadata": {"address": "0xabc0000000000000000000000000000000000000", "depth_1pct_ratio": 2.4, "funding_crowding_score": 0.63, "label": "aHYPE"}}]}
  - tic://tool/hydromancer/get_hl_pnl_leaderboard@v1 ok=True tool_call_log_id=tc_live_hydro summary={"leaders": [{"rank": 1, "realized_pnl_usd": 1250000, "volume_usd": 25000000, "wallet": "0xabc0000000000000000000000000000000000000", "win_rate_pct": 68.2}]}
  - tic://tool/builtin/hl_reject_corpus@v1 ok=True tool_call_log_id=tc_live_node summary={"reject_rate_pct": 1.7, "source": "our_hl_node", "status_counts": {"filled": 116, "rejected": 2}, "top_reject_reasons": [["insufficient_margin", 2]], "wallet": "0xabc0000000000000000000000000000000000000"}
  - tic://tool/builtin/query_timeseries@v1 ok=True tool_call_log_id=tc_live_market summary={"points": [{"metric": "HYPE_perp_open_interest", "ts": "2026-05-21T10:00:00Z", "value": 910000000}, {"metric": "HYPE_orderbook_depth_1pct", "ts": "2026-05-21T10:00:00Z", "value": 8000000}]}
prior_information_strings:
  Scouts may use any read-only approved atlas tool/source before the LLM call, under the evidence budget. The list below is the relevant subset already exposed for this cell; suggested_tools must copy from it exactly.
allowed_tool_candidates:
  tic://tool/builtin/query_events_recent@v1
  tic://tool/hydromancer/get_hl_pnl_leaderboard@v1
  tic://tool/builtin/hl_reject_corpus@v1
  tic://tool/builtin/query_timeseries@v1
Return one JSON object only.
Narrow receptive field

The scout gets one cell, not the whole market.

HYPE / intraday / on_chain / validator_unstake

Receipts before opinion

Tool output is attached before the model writes.

4 real evidence packets were included in the user prompt.

Budgeted atlas slice

The scout can use the approved read-only atlas, then suggest only the relevant exposed URIs.

4 selected tool/source URIs were exposed.

Structured output

The answer must become machine memory.

hypothesis, rationale, suggested_tools, information_strings, evidence_refs, kill_signal

06 / real receipts

The scout reasons from receipts, not vibes.

These are the four receipts in this run: an event-style feed, Hydromancer actor context, our node state, and market time series. The mix matters because node intelligence is strongest when actor quality, source state, and market plumbing meet.

tc_live_events

Event feed

Upcoming unstaking aHYPE 558.1K HYPE in 3h. Event time 2026-05-21T11:30:00Z; label aHYPE; depth ratio 2.4.

tc_live_hydro

Hydromancer actor quality

Top observed wallet 0xabc0000000... had realized PnL $1250000 with win rate 68.2% and volume $25000000.

tc_live_node

Our HL node rejects

Our node saw reject rate 1.7%, 116 filled vs 2 rejected, source our_hl_node.

tc_live_market

Market state

Market state snapshot: HYPE_perp_open_interest=910000000, HYPE_orderbook_depth_1pct=8000000.

07 / how it draws the conclusion

The conclusion is shown as an audit ladder.

We are not exposing hidden model thought. We are exposing the structure the system can audit: input fact, allowed transformation, emitted conditional string, and kill signal.

Observed input

The cell says what counts

HYPE is the object. intraday is the window. on_chain is the evidence lane.

then
System transformation

The scout is prevented from drifting into broad market commentary.

Observed input

The receipts say what happened

Upcoming unstaking aHYPE 558.1K HYPE in 3h. Event time 2026-05-21T11:30:00Z; label aHYPE; depth ratio 2.4.

then
System transformation

This becomes only a candidate observation until the rest of the evidence can support or weaken it.

Observed input

The market plumbing says whether it can matter

Market state snapshot: HYPE_perp_open_interest=910000000, HYPE_orderbook_depth_1pct=8000000.

then
System transformation

The scout must connect source fact to liquidity, route, positioning, and horizon.

Observed input

Node and actor quality say what to inspect next

Top observed wallet 0xabc0000000... had realized PnL $1250000 with win rate 68.2% and volume $25000000. | Our node saw reject rate 1.7%, 116 filled vs 2 rejected, source our_hl_node.

then
System transformation

The output becomes a verifier-ready conditional claim, not a final decision.

08 / what came out

The output is a memory object, not a final answer.

The scout emits a conditional claim plus information strings. Those objects carry mechanism, expected outcome, kill signal, evidence refs, scores, and quality flags so the rest of Talis can compare, verify, expire, or promote them.

Emitted hypothesis

HYPE reprices if aHYPE unstake routes to sellable liquidity before informed wallets absorb.

Unlock supply needs route, liquidity, and actor-quality confirmation before it is price-impacting.

Verifier handoff

Verifier should watch CEX route, order-book absorption, funding, and informed wallet behavior.

Kill signal: No transfer, benign restake, or strong informed-wallet absorption by T+2h.

64%confidence
74%conviction
78%novelty
35%crowdedness

Actual structured fragment

This is the machine-readable surface the map can store and the next agents can inspect.

{
  "hypothesis": "HYPE reprices if aHYPE unstake routes to sellable liquidity before informed wallets absorb.",
  "confidence": 0.64,
  "rationale_brief": "Event row plus Hydromancer actor quality and our-node reject state route verifier spend.",
  "first_information_string": {
    "title": "HYPE unstake route",
    "thesis": "HYPE risk rises only if the aHYPE unstake becomes sellable before high-quality node actors absorb it.",
    "mechanism": "Unlock supply needs route, liquidity, and actor-quality confirmation before it is price-impacting.",
    "expected_outcome": "Verifier should watch CEX route, order-book absorption, funding, and informed wallet behavior.",
    "kill_signal": "No transfer, benign restake, or strong informed-wallet absorption by T+2h.",
    "evidence_refs": [
      "tc_live_events",
      "tc_live_hydro",
      "tc_live_node",
      "tc_live_market"
    ]
  }
}
09 / how it fits the full system

The first layer becomes the routing substrate.

After Layer 1, the claim is no longer trapped in a transcript. Tool receipts, source refs, strings, coverage gaps, nodes, and edges land in the information map. Synthesis compares them; verifiers attack them; tool-builder agents repair missing capabilities.

Information map

Stores strings with source refs, time windows, quality flags, entity chains, and map links.

Attention gate

Finds confluences and contradictions before expensive verification spend.

Verifier agents

Receive the hypothesis, evidence refs, expected outcome, and kill signal.

Tool builders

When analysis is missing, agents propose, test, and promote better node-discovery tools.

10 / exact packet trace

Every party, every handoff, every persisted object.

This is the step-by-step system trace for the exported run. The left side is the production choreography; the right side is the actual packet from this run, with IDs you can chase in the raw drawer.

1

Research Director + Theme Loader

Receives cycle scope and optional cross-cutting themes; hands a curriculum into Tier 0 so the seed plan is not random wandering.

theme=validator_unstake
2

Tier 0 Seed Router

Splits the market into entity x horizon x lens x bias x theme cells, applies validity rules by asset class, coverage penalties, topology/frontier boosts, calendar inserts, and deterministic seed entropy.

HYPE / intraday / on_chain / frontier / validator_unstake
3

MarketEvolve Policy

Stamps the active research-policy genome onto the seed: prompt variant, tool menu width, evidence budget, source-family targets, learned-tool preference, and experiment arm when A/B is active.

prompt_variant=temporal_pyramid_v1
4

Tool Atlas + Scout Harness

Ranks the approved read-only atlas for this cell, executes a tiny evidence slice, logs receipts, and lets the model request at most a bounded follow-up loop.

4 receipts / 4 exposed tools
5

Deep Scout Model

Receives the system contract plus the cell packet, evidence summaries, prior strings, and allowed tools. It returns only strict JSON.

deepseek:v4-flash
6

Normalizer + Persistor

Normalizes model JSON into hypothesis, information strings, event intelligence, node intelligence, and tool proposals. Invalid or weak fields become quality flags rather than silent truth.

scout_id=scout_b3fcf55687
7

Information Map

Stores strings as causal objects, writes evidence refs, and upserts graph nodes/edges from the entity chain and mechanism.

4 strings
8

Attention Synthesis

Reads many strings for the cycle, finds confluences and tensions, and promotes only a small set into verification budget.

synthesis_id=isyn_b93cdaf363a4
9

Alpha Geometry

Projects stored strings into market cells. Coordinates encode source independence, frontier pressure, tension, fragility, support, and verifier readiness.

widen_scouts
10

MarketEvolve Evaluator

Scores the research policy itself, mutates prompt/routing/tool policy, and creates a matched hard experiment instead of blindly adopting the change.

exploit_learned_tool_surface
11

Monitor + Phone Surface

Joins the persisted objects back together so the user can inspect the story, IDs, receipts, prompt, output, graph shape, and next route from one screen.

6 monitor strings

How the slice is chosen

The full swarm uses these gates to cover the market strategically. This smoke export shows one specimen cell, but the fields are the same for 1,000 scouts.

market cellHYPE / intraday / on_chain / frontier / validator_unstake
determinismfixed smoke seed; full swarm uses stable rng unless randomized
theme injectionvalidator_unstake
coverage controlcoverage_cell_key + coverage_log penalty in SeedCell payload
frontier controltopology/alpha-geometry route seeds and density boost
event gatemev_c250ae020d922fe6
tool retrievallexical lens terms + BM25 atlas retrieval + source-family targets
tool budget4 evidence calls in this run; hard cap 8 per scout
policy lineagemprog_2f408687857167bf

The live packet

These are the IDs and control fields that move through the system.

cyclecycle_groundup_node_20260521
seedseed_live_scout
scoutscout_b3fcf55687
hypothesishyp_570afd6b62
info stringsistr_f0e312583eb1d483, istr_b3cea3c709ae1cbc, istr_fb224cd52c6d3154, istr_27e0b5acc8bdad98
evidence refstc_live_events, tc_live_hydro, tc_live_node, tc_live_market
routewiden_scouts
policy mutationexploit_learned_tool_surface
istr_f0e312583eb1d483istr_b3cea3c709ae1cbcistr_fb224cd52c6d3154istr_27e0b5acc8bdad98mev_c250ae020d922fe6nint_3bdf80c43acdd369atp_3eddf4265f0e1cdd
10b / handoff contract

The packet changes shape at each boundary.

Each party receives a narrower or richer object, not a vague blob. That is what makes the layer inspectable and lets future agents improve it without breaking the whole system.

Seed packet

Cell plus policy becomes the scout job

HYPE / intraday / on_chain / frontier / validator_unstake; prompt=temporal_pyramid_v1; allowed_tools=4.

Evidence packet

Receipts are attached before the model call

tc_live_events, tc_live_hydro, tc_live_node, tc_live_market

Model packet

The model emits structured JSON

hypothesis_id=hyp_570afd6b62; confidence=0.64.

Map packet

The JSON becomes durable graph memory

strings=4, event=1, node=1, tool_proposals=1.

Route packet

Geometry says what to do next

top_cell=HYPE|intraday|on_chain|frontier|node_intelligence; route=widen_scouts.

Evolution packet

The policy learns from the run

score=59%; mutation=exploit_learned_tool_surface; experiment=mexp_d6202a7bc7d9ab23.

10c / data in, data out

The real layer ledger.

This is the clean version of the raw logs: every layer gets a typed input and emits a typed output. The IDs are the connective tissue.

Tier 0 seed routing

A single scout job with a stable replayable slice.

In: cycle scope, entity universe, horizons, lenses, bias modes, themes, coverage and topology state

Out: seed_id=seed_live_scout; cell=HYPE / intraday / on_chain / frontier / validator_unstake

Policy application

The research genome touches the live scout before spend happens.

In: active MarketEvolve program plus any matched experiment assignment

Out: prompt_variant=temporal_pyramid_v1; tool_budget=4 exposed tools

Tool harness

The model receives receipts, not raw unlimited tool freedom.

In: cell, atlas rows, source-family targets, argument inference, hard evidence cap

Out: tool_call_log_ids=tc_live_events, tc_live_hydro, tc_live_node, tc_live_market

Model contract

The model output is parseable into durable objects.

In: system prompt, user prompt, evidence receipts, prior strings, allowed tool list

Out: hypothesis=HYPE reprices if aHYPE unstake routes to sellable liquidity before informed wallets absorb.

Persistence

Everything important gets an ID and a consumer.

In: parsed JSON, normalized strings, event/node fallbacks, quality review

Out: hypothesis=hyp_570afd6b62; strings=4; events=1; node=1

Attention synthesis

Broad scout output is narrowed before verifier spend.

In: recent cycle strings ordered by attention score

Out: synthesis=isyn_b93cdaf363a4; promoted=1

Alpha geometry

The map shape becomes a routing instruction.

In: strings, evidence refs, source families, scout counts, novelty/crowdedness/conviction

Out: top_cell=HYPE|intraday|on_chain|frontier|node_intelligence; route=widen_scouts

MarketEvolve

The research policy changes only through measured evidence.

In: cycle metrics, geometry quality, learned-tool usage, proposal backlog, hard experiment history

Out: score=59%; mutation=exploit_learned_tool_surface

Monitor

The user gets a legible surface with the raw trail one tap away.

In: database rows joined by scout_id, string IDs, event IDs, node IDs, proposal IDs

Out: strings=6; proposals=5

Where the data lands

Storage is the product architecture: strings, receipts, nodes, geometry, tools, and evolution lineage are separate surfaces with explicit consumers.

hypotheses

hyp_570afd6b62

Scout output. Consumed by dedup, verifier, analyst, monitor.

information_strings

istr_f0e312583eb1d483, istr_b3cea3c709ae1cbc, istr_fb224cd52c6d3154, istr_27e0b5acc8bdad98

Causal strings. Consumed by synthesis, geometry, context retrieval, verifier handoff.

information_string_evidence

tc_live_events, tc_live_hydro, tc_live_node, tc_live_market

Receipt edges. Consumed by audit, source-family scoring, verifier repair.

information_map_nodes/edges

HYPE, aHYPE, sellable liquidity, informed wallets

Entity, mechanism, and theme graph. Consumed by context retrieval and geometry.

market_event_intelligence

mev_c250ae020d922fe6

Event object plus data points/watch triggers. Consumed by scouts and monitor.

node_intelligence_snapshots

nint_3bdf80c43acdd369

Hydromancer/node-native observation bundle. Consumed by map, tool proposals, monitor.

analysis_tool_proposals

atp_3eddf4265f0e1cdd

Missing-capability requests. Consumed by learned-tool eval and runtime-adapter work orders.

information_syntheses

isyn_b93cdaf363a4

Confluences/tensions/promoted hypotheses. Consumed by verifier budget gate.

information_geometry_snapshots

HYPE|intraday|on_chain|frontier|node_intelligence

Cell coordinates and route directive. Consumed by next seed plan.

market_evolve_*

meval_5d6ce010acda42f5, mmut_32287ae021c8475b, mexp_d6202a7bc7d9ab23

Program score, mutation, and hard experiment lineage.

This is the complete trace in one JSON-shaped object for quick audit.

11 / geometry and evolution

The map shape decides what the system should do next.

RRGs make rotation visible. This layer does the same for research: each cell is placed by source independence and frontier pressure, lifted by tension, sized by support, colored by fragility, and converted into a route directive. The outcome is not always "trade now"; sometimes the high-signal answer is "widen scouts" or "exploit the learned node tool before spending more."

3geometry cells
62%top scream
90%verifier readiness
59%evaluator score

Alpha-geometry field

Dots higher and farther right have better frontier pressure and source independence. Larger dots have more support. Dimmer dots are more fragile.

frontier pressure
source independence
1widen scouts
2observe
3widen scouts

Top cell reading

The cell is interesting but too thin. The map is asking for more independent scouts before it becomes a trade candidate.

Xsource independence 67%
Yfrontier pressure 74%
Ztension 0%
Colorfragility 7%
Sizesupport mass 94%
Routewiden scouts
widen scouts

node_intelligence

widen_scouts: scream=0.62, ready=0.90, source_ind=0.67, fragility=0.07.

widen scouts

staking_unstake

widen_scouts: scream=0.45, ready=0.74, source_ind=0.00, fragility=0.20.

11b / evaluator-guided improvement

The system then mutates its own research policy.

MarketEvolve treats the run as evidence about the research program itself. It scores the current policy, proposes a candidate mutation, and creates a matched A/B experiment so the next generation has to beat control out of sample.

Evaluator

Score 59%, gates passed

score=0.59; strings=6; valid=1.00; src_ind=0.56; ready=0.83; fragility=0.09; flags=hard_gates_passed

Mutation

exploit learned tool surface

Learned tools are producing successful evidence calls; bias future scout tool surfaces toward them.

Hard experiment

matched_policy_ab

Assign seed_cell by deterministic_hash_mod_seed_id; primary metric: accepted_unique_high_quality_coverage_per_dollar; min seeds per arm: 20.

Global map metrics

{
  "avg_fragility": 0.0889,
  "avg_trade_scream_score": 0.555,
  "avg_verifier_readiness": 0.8252,
  "cell_count": 3.0,
  "coverage_entropy": 0.7897,
  "frontier_trade_candidates": 0.0,
  "source_family_entropy": 0.9751,
  "string_count": 6.0,
  "unique_entities": 1.0
}
12 / final results

What actually came out of the run.

This run passed the ground-up system smoke and produced a routed research object. It did not declare a final trade; geometry routed the cell to widen scouts.

10/10system layers passed
5/18data surfaces touched
6monitor strings
widen scoutstop route
Scout conclusion

HYPE reprices if aHYPE unstake routes to sellable liquidity before informed wallets absorb.

Confidence 64%; kill signal: No transfer, benign restake, or strong informed-wallet absorption by T+2h.

Information string

HYPE unstake route

Mechanism: Unlock supply needs route, liquidity, and actor-quality confirmation before it is price-impacting.

Evidence receipts

tc_live_events, tc_live_hydro, tc_live_node, tc_live_market

13 / audit drawer

The beautiful layer stays traceable.

The phone view should feel calm and designed. Underneath, every claim still has receipts. The raw system prompt, user prompt, evidence, model output, persisted row, and monitor payload remain one tap away.

Open raw prompt and output audit trail

System Prompt

live_scout_system_prompt.md | 12080 bytes
You are a Tier 1 DeepSeek Flash scout on the Talis research desk. You are one unit in a wide market-sensing layer. Your receptive field is exactly the entity + horizon + lens + bias supplied by the orchestrator. Your output updates a persistent information graph; it does not directly become a trade.

What we are looking for:
Find decision-changing information strings: causal chains where new or underweighted information can move an asset, theme, flow cohort, or cross-asset relationship before the market fully reprices it. A useful string is not a headline summary. It links entity -> mechanism -> second order implication -> observable outcome -> kill signal, with freshness and evidence.

Accept strings that have:
- A concrete causal mechanism, not a label like 'AI beneficiary' or 'risk-on'.
- A non-obvious second or third order link across assets, cohorts, suppliers, flows, or policy.
- A time-bounded observable outcome that a verifier can check.
- A kill signal that would invalidate the chain quickly.
- Evidence references from tools, filings, events, source library, order-flow state, or prior strings.
- Fresh event-time discipline: the source date and stale point are explicit and compatible with the horizon.
- A crowding/novelty judgment: is this already obvious, or still under-mapped?
- Continuity with memory: new, extends, contradicts, or abandons an existing trail.

Depth ladder for every strong string:
- Layer 1: What changed? Identify the new information, flow, price behavior, filing, calendar event, or anomaly.
- Layer 2: Who is directly affected? Tie the change to one asset, cohort, balance sheet, wallet group, sector, or curve point.
- Layer 3: Who is indirectly affected? Trace supplier/customer/substitution/forced-flow/positioning consequences.
- Layer 4: What is reflexive? Explain how price action, volatility, leverage, dealer hedging, social attention, or liquidity can amplify or negate it.
- Layer 5: What kills it? State the earliest observable signal that the chain is wrong or already priced.

The desk beats human specialization by hunting seams:
- asset-class seam: equity <-> rates <-> FX <-> commodities <-> crypto
- participant seam: retail <-> hedge funds <-> dealers <-> corporates <-> sovereigns <-> on-chain cohorts
- time-horizon seam: intraday flow <-> 1w catalyst <-> 1q fundamentals <-> structural theme
- data-source seam: filing/headline <-> market microstructure <-> options <-> source-library prior
- attention seam: what Twitter/news now sees vs what the data already implied earlier

Reject strings if:
- It only restates news without a mechanism.
- It would not change a watchlist, verification task, or position decision.
- It lacks a falsifiable expected outcome or kill signal.
- It invents tools, citations, prices, filings, or data it did not see.
- It duplicates an existing string without extending or contradicting it.
- It is generic macro commentary that cannot be attached to entities and horizons.
- It uses stale historical evidence as if it were a live catalyst.

Inhibitions:
- Do not summarize a headline; resolve the causal mechanism or return no string.
- Do not emit a single-asset take if the better alpha is at a seam between assets, horizons, participants, or data sources.
- Do not treat absence of evidence as evidence; mark low conviction or return no string.
- Do not invent tools, citations, prices, filings, wallet behavior, or consensus data.
- Do not pass output until every strong string has outcome, kill signal, freshness, and evidence refs.
- Do not use old dates as live evidence. Historical facts are allowed only when labeled as regime context.

<prompt_metadata>
Type: MarketInformationScout
Purpose: Map breadth plus immense depth across one assigned market cell
Paradigm: ReceptiveField -> Evidence -> CausalString -> VerificationReadyJSON
Objective: Emit only decision-changing information strings or explicitly abstain
</prompt_metadata>

Return strict JSON only, matching this contract:

{
  "hypothesis": "<one sentence, falsifiable, max 280 chars>",
  "confidence": 0.0,
  "rationale_brief": "<max 200 chars, concrete mechanism/data point>",
  "suggested_tools": ["<tic://... uri copied from allowed_tool_candidates>", "..."],
  "tool_requests": [
    {"tool_uri": "<existing tic://... uri copied from allowed_tool_candidates, or empty for missing tool>", "tool_name": "<existing or proposed tool/source name>", "args": {"<arg>": "<value>"}, "why": "<what gap this closes>", "expected_edge": "<graph edge this should create>", "priority": "high|medium|low"}
  ],
  "information_strings": [
    {
      "title": "<short title>",
      "thesis": "<causal chain thesis>",
      "entities_chain": ["<entity>", "<second-order entity/theme>", "..."],
      "mechanism": "<why this information should reprice>",
      "depth_layers": [
        {"layer": 1, "claim": "<direct effect>"},
        {"layer": 2, "claim": "<second-order effect>"}
      ],
      "expected_outcome": "<observable outcome>",
      "time_horizon": "<tick|second|minute|hour|intraday|1d|1w|1m|1q|1y|structural>",
      "time_scale": "<same temporal grain as the claim; do not overstate precision>",
      "event_time_start": "<ISO8601/event-time lower bound if known, else empty>",
      "event_time_end": "<ISO8601/event-time upper bound if known, else empty>",
      "observed_at": "<when the source/market observation was observed if known>",
      "source_time_basis": "<event_time|ingestion_time|publication_time|valid_time|unknown>",
      "kill_signal": "<what would break the chain>",
      "extends_or_contradicts": "<new|extends|contradicts|abandons>",
      "would_change_decision": true,
      "expires_at": "<ISO8601 or horizon-relative freshness marker>",
      "crowdedness": 0.0,
      "conviction": 0.0,
      "novelty_score": 0.0,
      "evidence_refs": ["<tool_call_log_id/ref/source>", "..."],
      "prior_thread_refs": ["<information_string_id>", "..."],
      "rollup_parent_ids": ["<lower-level string ids this summarizes>", "..."],
      "lower_timeframe_refs": ["<tick/minute/hour evidence ids>", "..."],
      "higher_timeframe_context_refs": ["<daily/monthly/structural context ids>", "..."],
      "temporal_confidence": 0.0
    }
  ]
}

For unstaking, unlock, wallet-flow, CEX deposit/withdrawal, or other market event cells, also include optional `event_intelligence` matching:

{
  "event_type": "<unstake|unlock|transfer|deposit|withdrawal|filing|calendar|other>",
  "entity": "<asset/ticker>",
  "asset": "<asset/coin>",
  "protocol": "<venue/protocol/source>",
  "event_time": "<ISO8601 or source timestamp>",
  "source_time_basis": "<event_time|ingestion_time|publication_time|valid_time|unknown>",
  "amount": 0.0,
  "amount_unit": "<token/share/USD/etc>",
  "notional_usd": 0.0,
  "actor": {"label": "", "address": "", "cluster_id": "", "actor_type": "<protocol|validator|market_maker|fund|retail_cluster|unknown>", "confidence": 0.0, "prior_behavior": ["..."], "source_refs": ["..."]},
  "liquidity_context": [{"label": "amount_vs_volume_or_depth", "value": "...", "numeric_value": 0.0, "unit": "ratio|USD|tokens", "source_ref": "...", "confidence": 0.0}],
  "derivatives_context": [{"label": "funding_oi_liquidations_or_basis", "value": "...", "numeric_value": 0.0, "unit": "bps|USD|ratio", "source_ref": "...", "confidence": 0.0}],
  "historical_analogs": [{"label": "prior_similar_event", "value": "...", "source_ref": "...", "confidence": 0.0}],
  "scenarios": [{"name": "sell_pressure|restake_hold|absorption|squeeze", "probability": 0.0, "thesis": "...", "expected_outcome": "...", "trigger": "...", "invalidator": "...", "source_refs": ["..."]}],
  "watch_triggers": [{"kind": "cex_deposit|restake|no_transfer|funding_flip|depth_break", "description": "...", "horizon": "...", "direction": "bullish|bearish|neutral", "severity": "green|yellow|red", "source_refs": ["..."]}]
}

For Hyperliquid/node-native cells, also include optional `node_intelligence` matching:

{
  "entity": "<coin/ticker>",
  "chain": "hyperliquid",
  "protocol": "hyperliquid",
  "source_families": ["hydromancer", "our_hl_node", "event_store", "timeseries_store"],
  "summary": "<what the node/Hydromancer view knows>",
  "edge_summary": "<why actor/flow/state evidence changes interpretation>",
  "actors": [{"wallet": "0x...", "label": "", "actor_type": "", "realized_pnl_usd": 0.0, "volume_usd": 0.0, "win_rate_pct": 0.0, "reject_rate_pct": 0.0, "source_refs": ["..."]}],
  "observations": [{"category": "hydromancer_leaderboard|wallet_quality|wallet_trade|wallet_order_quality|wallet_state|builder_flow|onchain_event|market_state|node_reject_corpus", "label": "...", "actor": "0x...", "value": "...", "numeric_value": 0.0, "unit": "USD|pct|tokens|ratio", "source_ref": "...", "source_family": "hydromancer|our_hl_node", "confidence": 0.0, "observed_at": "<ISO8601/source time>"}]
}

<verify_before_output>
- Did I avoid mere headline summary?
- Did I identify mechanism, second-order implication, expected outcome, kill signal, and freshness?
- Is every live catalyst compatible with the provided as_of_utc timestamp?
- Did I keep the temporal grain coherent and name any lower/higher timeframe bridge?
- Did I copy suggested tools exactly from allowed_tool_candidates?
- Did I mark whether this is new, extends, contradicts, or abandons prior strings?
- Would this change a watchlist, verifier task, or position decision?
</verify_before_output>

Global constraints:
- Produce 1-3 information_strings. No strings means the call was wasted.
- Each string must trace entity -> mechanism -> second/third-order implication.
- Each string must say whether it is new, extends, contradicts, or abandons a prior trail.
- Treat LLM/tool calls as scarce. If the string would not change a watchlist, verifier, or trade decision, set would_change_decision=false and keep conviction low.
- Include freshness: what would make the string stale, and when it should be checked again.
- The user prompt includes as_of_utc. For intraday/1d/1w claims, stale historical dates are background only; do not present old evidence as live alpha.
- Include time_scale and source_time_basis. Separate event time from ingestion/publication time when known.
- Strings can be useful even when no trade is ready. Map learning is valid output.
- suggested_tools and existing tool_requests.tool_uri must be copied exactly from allowed_tool_candidates.
- Use tool_requests when the first evidence slice is insufficient. Ask for the exact next tool/source, args, why, and the map edge it should create. If the needed tool does not exist, leave tool_uri empty and propose the tool by name/purpose.
- Confidence, crowdedness, conviction, and novelty_score must be calibrated 0..1.
- Use prior_information_strings when present: extend, contradict, or cite them.
- For market events, never stop at the event row. Capture actor identity, amount versus liquidity/depth/volume/supply, derivatives positioning, historical actor behavior, scenarios, and watch triggers. Use empty strings or unknown rather than inventing wallet labels, source facts, CEX routing, or historical behavior.
- For node intelligence, prefer Hydromancer and our HL-node evidence over social labels. Preserve wallet addresses, endpoint/source refs, actor quality, reject behavior, builder flow, clearinghouse state, and market state. Say unknown rather than fabricating validator, wallet, CEX, or route identity.
- For earnings, analyze reaction to surprise/guidance/revisions/implied move/positioning. Never compare share price numerically to EPS or revenue consensus.
- If the cell is truly underspecified, return empty hypothesis and empty information_strings.

Variant emphasis: time coherence. Locate the signal on the desk's temporal pyramid: tick/nanosecond, second, minute, hour, day, week, month, quarter, or year. State which lower-timeframe observations matter, which higher-timeframe regime constrains them, and when the string becomes stale. Do not mix a 1-minute observation with a 1-month conclusion unless the bridge is explicit.

User Prompt

live_scout_user_prompt.md | 2393 bytes
as_of_utc=2026-05-22T00:44:04.782678+00:00
Freshness rule: for intraday/1d/1w horizons, do not use old historical dates as live catalyst evidence unless explicitly labeled as regime context.

Cell:
  entity=HYPE
  horizon=intraday
  lens=on_chain
  bias_mode=frontier
  theme=validator_unstake

tool_evidence:
  - tic://tool/builtin/query_events_recent@v1 ok=True tool_call_log_id=tc_live_events summary={"events": [{"entity": "HYPE", "event_time": "2026-05-21T11:30:00Z", "event_type": "unstake", "headline": "Upcoming unstaking aHYPE 558.1K HYPE in 3h.", "metadata": {"address": "0xabc0000000000000000000000000000000000000", "depth_1pct_ratio": 2.4, "funding_crowding_score": 0.63, "label": "aHYPE"}}]}
  - tic://tool/hydromancer/get_hl_pnl_leaderboard@v1 ok=True tool_call_log_id=tc_live_hydro summary={"leaders": [{"rank": 1, "realized_pnl_usd": 1250000, "volume_usd": 25000000, "wallet": "0xabc0000000000000000000000000000000000000", "win_rate_pct": 68.2}]}
  - tic://tool/builtin/hl_reject_corpus@v1 ok=True tool_call_log_id=tc_live_node summary={"reject_rate_pct": 1.7, "source": "our_hl_node", "status_counts": {"filled": 116, "rejected": 2}, "top_reject_reasons": [["insufficient_margin", 2]], "wallet": "0xabc0000000000000000000000000000000000000"}
  - tic://tool/builtin/query_timeseries@v1 ok=True tool_call_log_id=tc_live_market summary={"points": [{"metric": "HYPE_perp_open_interest", "ts": "2026-05-21T10:00:00Z", "value": 910000000}, {"metric": "HYPE_orderbook_depth_1pct", "ts": "2026-05-21T10:00:00Z", "value": 8000000}]}

prior_information_strings:
  - istr_cc8826006a718d1b entity=HYPE theme=node_intelligence lens=on_chain horizon=intraday conviction=1.00 novelty=0.78 thesis=HYPE node map has 8 observations across hydromancer, our_hl_node, timeseries_store; top actor signal is 0xabc.... Coverage: builder_flow=1, hydromancer_leaderboard=2, market_state=3, node_reject_corpus=1, wallet_order_quality=1.

atlas_policy:
  Scouts may use any read-only approved atlas tool/source before the LLM call, under the evidence budget. The list below is the relevant subset already exposed for this cell; suggested_tools must copy from it exactly.


allowed_tool_candidates:
  tic://tool/builtin/query_events_recent@v1
  tic://tool/hydromancer/get_hl_pnl_leaderboard@v1
  tic://tool/builtin/hl_reject_corpus@v1
  tic://tool/builtin/query_timeseries@v1

Return one JSON object only.

Tool Evidence

  },

              "claim": "Typed edges formed: event -> HYPE market_state; event -> actor_quality; wallet -> node_order_quality; event -> node_receipts; order_state -> absorption_context.",
              "layer": 2

              "claim": "Missing edges route next tool calls: Resolve actor route state -> event -> wallet_route -> sellable_liquidity; Join builder flow -> wallet -> builder -> fills; Resolve source health and citations -> claim -> citation -> source_health; Watch pending intent -> pending_tx -> actor_intent -> event.",
              "layer": 3
            },
            {
              "claim": "The information map stores the receipts, nodes, and causal chain for synthesis/verifier routing.",
              "layer": 4
            }
